# @vllnt/convex-tokens

> Hashed-secret token primitive with mint-once, TTL, revoke and scope, as a Convex component

`@vllnt/convex-tokens` is a publishable Convex component. It targets `convex@^1.36.1`.
It stores one sandboxed table - `tokens` - holding only the digest of each token (never the raw secret), plus `scope`, an optional opaque `resourceRef`, a `revoked` flag, `expiresAt`, and `createdAt`. The client generates the raw token and hashes it with Web Crypto (SHA-256 by default, SHA-512 configurable per mount) before any call, so the component never sees the secret; minting returns the raw value exactly once. Time is server-sourced: the component computes `expiresAt` from a requested `ttlMs` (clamped server-side into a min/max range; a non-finite `ttlMs` falls back to the default) and reads `Date.now()` inside `validate`, so expiry cannot be forged by a client clock. The `mint` mutation enforces a server-side hash guard: it rejects any `tokenHash` that is not a lowercase hex string of at least 64 characters, throwing `ConvexError({ code: "INVALID_TOKEN_HASH" })`. The `validate` result is a discriminated union: `{ valid: true, resourceRef? }` or `{ valid: false }` (the failed branch structurally cannot carry `resourceRef`). `revoke` uses a transition contract: returns `true` only if this call transitioned the token from active to revoked; a second call returns `false`. The `list` query caps results at 1000 rows. Tokens can be revoked; a built-in daily cron prunes expired and stale-revoked rows in bounded self-rescheduling batches, and `prune` is also callable directly. A safe management surface (`list`, `getMetadata`) returns token metadata - never the hash. The host owns the resource, auth, and meaning; the component owns only the hash, lifecycle, and expiry.
